data.content:
The problem is clear. The target generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so MSBuild doesn't include it in compilation.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to the `@(Compile)` items within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds it to the `@(Compile)` item group. The file is written to disk but MSBuild's compilation step doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<ItemGroup>` inside the target:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the `WriteCodeFile` task call in the `GenerateSampleCode` target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. 

**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild only compiles files it knows about through item groups, so the generated file was ignored by the compiler.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="..." />` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
